record.field: Zammad is an open-source, web-based help desk that unifies email, chat and social channels into one collaborative ticketing workspace for support teams.

Zammad is an open-source, web-based help desk that unifies email, chat and social channels into one collaborative ticketing workspace for support teams.

Zammad is a full-featured customer support and ticketing platform, positioned as a self-hostable alternative to Zendesk and Freshdesk. It consolidates email, live chat, phone (CTI), Telegram, Facebook and X into a single shared inbox, so agents work every conversation from one place with a complete customer history. Built on Ruby on Rails with an Elasticsearch-powered search, it is aimed at support, service and IT teams that want a professional help desk while keeping tickets and customer data on their own infrastructure.

Key features
  • Omnichannel shared inbox: email, live chat widget, phone/CTI, Telegram, Facebook and X
  • Full ticketing with SLAs, triggers, schedulers and automated escalations
  • Fast full-text search across tickets, users and organizations via Elasticsearch
  • Granular roles and permissions, plus a customizable knowledge base
  • Reporting, dashboards and a complete audit trail of every ticket change
  • REST API, webhooks and integrations (GitHub, GitLab, Slack, i-doit) for automation
Best for

Support, service and IT teams that need a professional, self-hosted help desk with omnichannel intake and full data ownership. Note: this is a multi-service stack (Elasticsearch needs RAM and vm.max_map_count=262144 on the host); create the first admin from the web UI after install, set the correct FQDN, and configure inbound/outbound email (DNS/MX/SMTP/IMAP) before going live.

services: zammad-postgresql: image: postgres:17-alpine environment: POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB} POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} volumes: - ./zammad_postgresql_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U "$$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$$POSTGRES_DB" interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgresql' expose: - '5432' networks: - internal zammad-redis: image: redis:7.4-alpine command: redis-server --appendonly yes volumes: - ./zammad_redis_data:/data healthcheck: test: - CMD - redis-cli - ping interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' expose: - '6379' networks: - internal zammad-memcached: image: memcached:1.6-alpine command: memcached -m 256M restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_memcached' expose: - '11211' networks: - internal zammad-elasticsearch: image: docker.io/bitnamilegacy/elasticsearch:8.17.4 environment: BITNAMI_DEBUG: 'false' ELASTICSEARCH_ENABLE_SECURITY: 'true' ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD: ${ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD} ELASTICSEARCH_SKIP_TRANSPORT_TLS: 'true' ELASTICSEARCH_ENABLE_REST_TLS: 'false' ELASTICSEARCH_HEAP_SIZE: 512m volumes: - ./zammad_elasticsearch_data:/bitnami/elasticsearch/data restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_elasticsearch' expose: - '9200' networks: - internal zammad-init: image: ghcr.io/zammad/zammad:latest command: - zammad-init user: 0:0 environment: &zammad_env MEMCACHE_SERVERS: zammad-memcached:11211 REDIS_URL: redis://zammad-redis:6379 POSTGRESQL_HOST: zammad-postgresql POSTGRESQL_PORT: '5432' POSTGRESQL_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB} POSTGRESQL_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER} POSTGRESQL_PASS: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} ELASTICSEARCH_HOST: zammad-elasticsearch ELASTICSEARCH_PORT: '9200' ELASTICSEARCH_SCHEMA: http ELASTICSEARCH_USER: elastic ELASTICSEARCH_PASS: ${ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD} depends_on: zammad-postgresql: condition: service_healthy volumes: - ./zammad_storage:/opt/zammad/storage restart: on-failure container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_init' networks: - internal zammad-railsserver: image: ghcr.io/zammad/zammad:latest command: - zammad-railsserver environment: *zammad_env depends_on: zammad-init: condition: service_completed_successfully zammad-postgresql: condition: service_healthy zammad-redis: condition: service_healthy zammad-memcached: condition: service_started zammad-elasticsearch: condition: service_started volumes: - ./zammad_storage:/opt/zammad/storage restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_railsserver' expose: - '3000' networks: - internal zammad-scheduler: image: ghcr.io/zammad/zammad:latest command: - zammad-scheduler environment: *zammad_env depends_on: zammad-init: condition: service_completed_successfully zammad-railsserver: condition: service_started volumes: - ./zammad_storage:/opt/zammad/storage restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_scheduler' networks: - internal zammad-websocket: image: ghcr.io/zammad/zammad:latest command: - zammad-websocket environment: *zammad_env depends_on: zammad-init: condition: service_completed_successfully zammad-railsserver: condition: service_started volumes: - ./zammad_storage:/opt/zammad/storage restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_websocket' expose: - '6042' networks: - internal zammad-nginx: image: ghcr.io/zammad/zammad:latest command: - zammad-nginx environment: *zammad_env depends_on: zammad-railsserver: condition: service_started zammad-websocket: condition: service_started volumes: - ./zammad_storage:/opt/zammad/storage restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR. # Zammad guarda los adjuntos en la BD por defecto, así que el dump es la fuente de verdad. docker compose exec -T zammad-postgresql sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d zammad-postgresql for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T zammad-postgresql sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T zammad-postgresql sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T zammad-postgresql sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
PostgreSQL role that owns the Zammad database.
Name of the PostgreSQL database used by Zammad.
Password for the built-in Elasticsearch 'elastic' user used for search.
